reviews.review_body: A well-organized school that encourages both academic excellence and overall development.

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.review_body:
Here’s a **well-written positive review** you can use or post:

---

**⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Excellent School with a Holistic Approach**

Rockwell International School offers a wonderful learning environment that focuses on the overall development of children. The school strikes a great balance between academics, extracurricular activities, and life skills, helping students grow into confident and well-rounded individuals.

The teachers are highly dedicated, caring, and approachable. They pay individual attention to students and encourage curiosity, creativity, and critical thinking. The curriculum is engaging and thoughtfully designed to make learning enjoyable rather than stressful.

The campus and facilities are impressive—spacious classrooms, well-equipped labs, sports facilities, and activity areas create a positive and stimulating atmosphere. Safety and security measures are also well maintained, giving parents peace of mind.

What truly sets Rockwell apart is its nurturing and supportive culture. Students are encouraged to explore their talents, express themselves freely, and develop strong values along with academic excellence. Overall, Rockwell International School is an excellent choice for parents looking for quality education with a holistic vision.

reviews.author: Pratibha
reviews.review_body:
I would like to share some genuine feedback with the intention of helping the school create an even more positive and encouraging environment for students.
While the school circulates information about external competitions, beyond this there is unfortunately no encouragement or guidance provided by the school. The entire responsibility is left to the child and parents. Even when a child gets selected or performs well, there is no follow-up support, guidance for next steps, or even basic appreciation or congratulations. A little motivation and acknowledgment from the school can go a long way in boosting a child’s confidence.
I would like to clarify that I have no complaints regarding the Principal. However, I am quite disappointed with the approach of the Middle School Coordinator, Madam Sharavanya. The general impression given is that a “good child” is one who sits quietly, while children who are talkative, expressive, or energetic are labeled as naughty or indisciplined. Sports and playtime are often cancelled if children are seen talking or enjoying themselves.
Children are children — they learn, grow, and express themselves through interaction and play. When a child is performing well academically, co-curricular, and overall, it is disheartening to see their natural enthusiasm and childhood joy being suppressed. School should be a place children love coming to, not a place that feels restrictive or suffocating.
Active, intelligent, and smart children are often extroverts. Their ability to communicate, express opinions, and engage socially should be seen as strengths, not flaws. Expecting children to behave like adults by being silent and passive takes away their childhood and impacts their emotional well-being.
We strongly feel that a coordinator should be child-friendly, approachable, and supportive. Unfortunately, most responses to parents’ requests are negative, which was not the case with the earlier coordinator, Ms. Saama Ma’am. Children today appear genuinely scared rather than comfortable approaching the coordinator, which is concerning.
I sincerely request the school management to please look into this matter and consider appointing coordinators and teachers who understand a child’s perspective, encourage talent, motivate students, and celebrate their achievements. Positive guidance and appreciation are essential, especially for children who are doing well and excelling in multiple areas.
Please do not take away a child’s childhood. Let school be a space where children feel safe, happy, motivated, and inspired to grow.
Thank you for your time and consideration. I hope this feedback will be taken in the right spirit for the betterment of the children and the school.

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.review_body: Good school with lot of interdisciplinary activities

reviews.author: Prashanth Reddy
reviews.review_body: my daughter joined 6th Grade last year. As usual, adjusting to a new environment who shifted from US was worrying some. The way teachers and their COD Shravanya helped my daughter to settle in school is worth appreciation. The COD is one phone call away to solve all the concerns and support the child to take the right decision. The guidance that we received from school, with the help of Ms Shravanya, to choose the curriculum for grades 7 to 8 was huge. with patience, they heard our future plans for the child and guided us to. Well-balanced school without any frills.

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.review_body: A very good school . Good learning experience

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.review_body:
I am highly disappointed with the behaviour of the Middle School Coordinator, Ms. Shravanya. It has been observed that she often targets a few specific children and does not allow them to share their side of the story. Her approach usually involves raising her voice, insulting, or punishing the child, which is very disheartening.

I sincerely request the school management to kindly look into this matter. No child at Rockwell should ever go through mental distress or feel discouraged. We have all heard of unfortunate incidents like that of Amaira from Neerja Modi School, and I truly hope such situations never arise here.

A coordinator or teacher should be patient, understanding, and empathetic. Children naturally have high energy levels — this should be guided and channelled positively, not handled through scolding or humiliation. Having energy and enthusiasm is a sign of a healthy, active mind, not a fault.

My child is very intelligent but is repeatedly being singled out by Ms. Shravanya, which has become extremely concerning for us as parents. I kindly urge the school to ensure that teachers and coordinators are trained to handle students with patience and to motivate them positively, as is done in many schools abroad. Constant punishment and criticism can deeply affect a young and tender mind.

reviews.author: arun
reviews.review_body: Very nice school and children can learn many things from that school. Co-curricular activities are well. Teachers are flexible in answering questions. I from my side recommend the school for child admission
